title: 1.1 Practice
description: Practice what you learned in lesson 1.1

hints: Big History asks the big questions that cultures have been asking for thousands of years.
Humans and human thought play an important role in Big History.

choices.content: An attempt to explain the beginning of humanity and the Universe
A scientific explanation for how atoms formed
A theory as to why the Universe is expanding
The use of spoken language to pass down a people’s history

assessment_items.question_content:
**How is Big History different from other origin stories?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Big History begins with the Big Bang, 13.8 billion years ago.
Big History extends from the Big Bang to the future.

choices.content: Big History is a modern origin story based on scientific information from multiple disciplines.
Big History is a nationalist origin story and doesn’t consider global perspectives.
Big History focuses on the origin story of humans as the major agents of change.
Big History bases its story on mythology, ancient narratives, and oral history.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why are thresholds of increasing complexity important in Big History?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Claim testers, such as authority, logic, evidence, and authority, are used to evaluate information.
When looking at things from different scales, you can see differences in time, space, or size.

choices.content: They explain times when something entirely new appeared in the Universe that greatly impacted events that followed.
They show how humans evolved from a primate ancestor to increasingly complex humans.
They show us what happened every billion years since the Big Bang.
They explain how the Universe and stars formed after the Big Bang.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Big History switches between different perspectives of time and space in order to tell the history of the Universe. What term is used to describe these perspectives?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Origin stories are a narrative about the beginning of the Universe and humanity.
Scales provide context for degrees of magnification, or perspective, used to measure time, space, and size.

choices.content: Scale
Parallax
Astrophysics
Cosmology

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why is the Big Bang a good starting point for the history of the Universe?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: These are the “just right” conditions needed to create more complex things in the Universe.
Gravity draws matter together, bringing about interactions, which potentially lead to complexity.

choices.content: It’s when space and time (spacetime) were created.
It’s when the Earth was created.
It’s when life began.
It’s when the first stars appeared.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the history of the Universe, there are moments when specific ingredients under the right Goldilocks Conditions transform into something entirely new and more complex. What do we call these moments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Thresholds of increasing complexity
Claim testers
Collective learning
Scale
The “just right” conditions that, when combined with the necessary ingredients, can lead to new complexity.
The specific and diverse ingredients needed for new complexity to emerge.
The human ability to create complex, symbolic language.
The ability to look at things from different perspectives of time and space.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why are complex things more fragile than simple things?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Complex things are very precisely arranged and usually require more energy to maintain, which makes them more fragile than simple things.
Complex things are rare and only exist in certain parts of the Universe.
Complex things require far less energy than simple things.
Complex things only exist for very short periods of time.
